# Artificial intelligence and the future of testing

Creator: 
Date: 1990-01-01T00:00:00Z
Downloads: 59
Subjects: Educational tests and measurements -- United States -- Congresses, Artificial intelligence -- Educational applications -- Congresses, Computer-assisted instruction -- United States -- Congresses, Computer-Assisted Instruction, Tests et mesures en éducation -- États-Unis -- Congrès

xiv, 329 pages : 24 cm Includes bibliographical references and indexes Artificial intelligence and its implications for the future of ETS's tests / Roy Freedle -- From interactive instruction to interactive testing / Tryg A. Ager -- The Teacher's Apprentice Project : building an algebra tutor / Robert Milson, Matthew W. Lewis, and John R. Anderson -- A theory of graph comprehension / Steven Pinker -- What computers can see : a sketch of accomplishments in computer vision, with speculations on its use in educational testing / Leslie Kitchen -- Issues for a theory of analogical learning / Mark Burstein and Beth Adelson -- The access and use of relevant information : a specific case and general issues / Brian H. Ross -- Modeling software design within a problem-space architecture / Beth Adelson -- Memory organization and retrieval : an artificial intelligence perspective / Lisa F. Rau -- Two hurdles for natural language systems / Paul S. Jacobs Comments on artificial intelligence research : related issues in education and testing / Saul Amarel -- Communicative functions in interactions in natural language and other modalities / Aravind K. Joshi -- Assessment of an expert system's ability to grade and diagnose automatically student's constructed responses to computer science problems / Randy Elliot Bennett [and others]
